sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, json_data JSON, created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, json_data, created_at) VALUES (1, '[{"key": "value1"}, {"key": "value2"}]', '2023-10-01 12:00:00');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, json_data, created_at) VALUES (2, '[{"key": "value3"}]', '2023-10-02 12:00:00');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, json_data, created_at) VALUES (3, NULL, '2023-10-03 12:00:00');
SELECT row_count() FROM (UPDATE test_table SET json_data = '[{"key": "new_value"}]' WHERE id = 1);
SELECT row_count() FROM (DELETE FROM test_table WHERE json_array_length(json_data) = 1);
SELECT id, json_array_length(json_data) AS json_length FROM test_table WHERE json_length > 0 GROUP BY id HAVING json_length IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table t2 WHERE t1.id = t2.id) AS inner_count, json_array_length(json_data) FROM test_table t1 WHERE json_data IS NOT NULL;
UPDATE test_table SET json_data = '[{"key": "updated_value"}]' WHERE id = 2 RETURNING row_count();
SELECT (SELECT row_count() FROM (DELETE FROM test_table WHERE id = 3) AS inner_delete) + json_array_length(json_data) AS combined_result FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
